meanings.meaning: An outstanding person or thing
meanings.context: Roaring Twenties / Jazz Age slang for excellence

meanings.meaning: Extremely drunk
meanings.context: 1920s Prohibition-era slang

meanings.meaning: Something splendid or stylish; the best of the best
meanings.context: 1920s flapper culture, similar to 'bee's knees'

meanings.meaning: Neat and stylish in dress and appearance
meanings.context: 1920s high-society fashion culture

meanings.meaning: Alcoholic beverage, especially illicit liquor
meanings.context: 1920s Prohibition era — alcohol was illegal so euphemisms flourished

meanings.meaning: A feeling of anxiety, nervousness, or fear
meanings.context: Coined by cartoonist Billy DeBeck in 1923

meanings.meaning: Illicit or cheap liquor
meanings.context: From the Hoochinoo people of Alaska who made liquor; widespread during Prohibition

meanings.meaning: Okay, fine, satisfactory
meanings.context: 1920s general slang for approval

meanings.meaning: Attractive, appealing, or wonderful
meanings.context: 1920s youth culture

meanings.meaning: A gangster's girlfriend or female companion
meanings.context: 1920s organized crime culture; from the name Molly

meanings.meaning: Leave immediately; get out of here
meanings.context: 1920s underworld slang

meanings.meaning: An illicit bar or nightclub selling alcohol during Prohibition
meanings.context: 1920s Prohibition era — you had to 'speak easy' (quietly) about them

meanings.meaning: Having a great time; celebrating boisterously
meanings.context: 1920s party culture; also a euphemism for intimacy

meanings.meaning: The genuine article; the real thing
meanings.context: Disputed origin — possibly from bootlegger Bill McCoy who sold uncut liquor

meanings.meaning: Flawless, perfect, pleasing
meanings.context: 1920s coined by columnist Billy Rose

meanings.meaning: Get going; leave quickly; scram
meanings.context: Early 1900s–1920s; one of the earliest American slang catchphrases

meanings.meaning: An attractive woman
meanings.context: 1920s–1950s, common in film noir and flapper culture

meanings.meaning: Legs, especially a woman's legs
meanings.context: 1920s showbiz/entertainment culture

meanings.meaning: Completely wrong or mistaken
meanings.context: 1930s Great Depression era

meanings.meaning: Prison
meanings.context: 1930s gangster culture / crime films

meanings.meaning: Courage, nerve, determination
meanings.context: 1930s — from the Moxie brand soft drink that claimed to give energy

meanings.meaning: Elegant, fancy, high-class
meanings.context: 1930s — from the Ritz-Carlton hotels
meanings.tone: Admiring or sarcastic depending on context

meanings.meaning: An FBI agent or government law enforcement officer
meanings.context: 1930s — popularized during the gangster era; 'G' for Government

meanings.meaning: Tough, no-nonsense, unemotional
meanings.context: 1930s detective fiction / film noir culture

meanings.meaning: Nonsense; something untrue or foolish
meanings.context: 1930s general slang

meanings.meaning: A scapegoat; someone easily duped or blamed
meanings.context: 1930s crime/gangster culture

meanings.meaning: Something or someone wonderful; an excellent thing

meanings.meaning: A despicable or worthless person
meanings.context: 1930s insult slang

meanings.meaning: Impressive, powerful; a hit
meanings.context: 1930s showbiz/entertainment culture

meanings.meaning: A safecracker or burglar
meanings.context: 1930s underworld/crime slang

meanings.meaning: Annoyed, angry, frustrated
meanings.context: 1930s British slang that crossed the Atlantic

meanings.meaning: Stylish, sophisticated, composed; excellent
meanings.context: Derived from 1940s jazz culture, particularly Black musicians like Lester Young and Miles Davis. Oxford traces first use to 1933

meanings.meaning: Acceptable or fine ('that's cool with me')
meanings.context: Evolved from the original jazz meaning into general approval

meanings.meaning: A huge success; originally a large aerial bomb
meanings.context: 1940s WWII military slang — a bomb big enough to destroy a city block

meanings.meaning: An extremely attractive woman
meanings.context: 1940s Hollywood culture — Jean Harlow was 'the Blonde Bombshell'

meanings.meaning: Language that is meaningless or hard to understand; bureaucratic jargon

meanings.meaning: Popular slang or talk related to jazz culture
meanings.context: 1940s jazz/swing culture, particularly Black American communities

meanings.meaning: To mislead or deceive with insincere talk
meanings.context: Extended meaning from the original jazz sense

meanings.meaning: Something excellent, impressive, or thrilling
meanings.context: 1940s jazz/swing culture

meanings.meaning: Eyes; also slang for eyeglasses
meanings.context: 1940s general slang, common in detective fiction

meanings.meaning: Smooth, impressive, cleverly done
meanings.context: 1940s jazz culture / general approval

meanings.meaning: An important or influential person; the boss

meanings.meaning: A false accusation or unfair punishment
meanings.context: 1940s crime/legal slang

meanings.meaning: Cowardly; a coward

meanings.meaning: To leave quickly; to run away
meanings.context: 1940s military slang — soldiers moving fast

meanings.meaning: To lose control of your emotions; to go crazy

meanings.meaning: To lose your temper; to get angry suddenly
meanings.context: 1940s military/general slang

meanings.meaning: Excellent, first-class, top quality

meanings.meaning: A term of address, similar to 'man' or 'dude'
meanings.context: 1950s Beat Generation / jazz culture — from 'daddy' (mentor/cool person)

meanings.meaning: To understand, appreciate, or enjoy something
meanings.context: 1950s Beat Generation / jazz culture, from African American English

meanings.meaning: A boring or extremely dull place or situation
meanings.context: 1950s — adding '-ville' suffix was a popular slang trend

meanings.meaning: Cool, stylish, aware of what's trendy
meanings.context: 1950s jazz/beatnik culture — predates 'hippie' which derives from it

meanings.meaning: Looking for trouble; heading toward a fight

meanings.meaning: A punch in the face

meanings.meaning: Successful; assured of success; having it easy
meanings.context: 1950s general slang — shade meaning comfort/leisure

meanings.meaning: Clothes, especially stylish ones
meanings.context: 1950s jazz/hipster culture; still used today in some circles

meanings.meaning: Someone's house or apartment; a place to live
meanings.context: 1950s Beat Generation slang

meanings.meaning: Making out in the back seat of a car
meanings.context: 1950s teen/drive-in culture

meanings.meaning: A small child
meanings.context: 1950s family slang — kids being short enough to bite ankles

meanings.meaning: Dressed up; wearing fancy or stylish clothes
meanings.context: 1950s fashion culture

meanings.meaning: Kissing and making out
meanings.context: 1950s dating culture; considered mildly scandalous at the time

meanings.meaning: To accelerate a car quickly, making tires squeal
meanings.context: 1950s hot rod / car culture

meanings.meaning: A worthless or broken-down car or object
meanings.context: 1950s car culture — everyone judged your ride

meanings.meaning: A boring, no-fun person; a killjoy
meanings.context: 1950s social slang

meanings.meaning: The truth; reliable information
meanings.context: 1950s — 'the bird' implied an authoritative source

meanings.meaning: Cool, fantastic, wonderful

meanings.meaning: Amazing, cool, mind-blowing
meanings.context: 1960s hippie/counterculture slang — something so good it's 'far out' of the ordinary

meanings.meaning: The police

meanings.meaning: A person embracing the counterculture movement — peace, love, free expression
meanings.context: 1960s San Francisco / counterculture movement — derived from 'hip'

meanings.meaning: To selfishly keep something to yourself, especially a joint

meanings.meaning: Money
meanings.context: 1960s counterculture slang

meanings.meaning: Relax, take it easy, don't stress

meanings.meaning: Excellent, impressive, amazing
meanings.context: 1960s–1970s counterculture / jazz-influenced

meanings.meaning: Excellent, genuine, morally good
meanings.context: 1960s counterculture — used to describe something truly authentic

meanings.meaning: To leave a place
meanings.context: 1960s beatnik/hippie slang

meanings.meaning: A drug-induced experience, especially on psychedelics
meanings.context: 1960s drug culture / counterculture

meanings.meaning: A strange, surreal, or mind-bending situation

meanings.meaning: Short for fabulous; wonderful, excellent

meanings.meaning: A lively party or gathering
meanings.context: 1960s — from a popular TV music show called 'Shindig!'

meanings.meaning: A snitch, informer, or untrustworthy person

meanings.meaning: To show up somewhere; to be present at a social event

meanings.meaning: Tell me; give me the information or truth

meanings.meaning: Relaxed, calm, easygoing
meanings.context: 1960s counterculture — valued state of being

meanings.meaning: Excited, thrilled, energized

meanings.meaning: To dance, especially energetically
meanings.context: 1970s disco culture — from boogie-woogie music

meanings.meaning: A disappointment; a bad or depressing experience

meanings.meaning: Someone exhausted or lacking motivation, often from drug use

term: catch you on the flip side

meanings.meaning: See you later; goodbye
meanings.context: 1970s — 'flip side' referred to the B-side of a vinyl record

meanings.meaning: Great, awesome, exciting
meanings.context: 1970s — popularized by the TV show 'Good Times' (J.J.'s catchphrase 'Dy-no-mite!')

meanings.meaning: Attractive, especially a woman
meanings.context: 1970s — from 'fox' meaning an attractive person, popularized by Jimi Hendrix's 'Foxy Lady'

meanings.meaning: Stylish or cool in an unconventional, groove-heavy way
meanings.context: 1970s — from funk music culture and Black American expression

meanings.meaning: Excited, enthusiastic, pumped up
meanings.context: 1970s general slang

meanings.meaning: Expression of strong agreement; 'exactly!' or 'yes!'
meanings.context: 1970s counterculture and Black Power movement

meanings.meaning: To relax completely and do nothing; be a couch potato
meanings.context: 1970s — comparing yourself to an inactive vegetable

meanings.meaning: Do me a favor
meanings.context: 1970s general slang — a 'solid' meaning a firm, real favor

meanings.meaning: Continue persisting; don't give up
meanings.context: 1970s — from Bob Dylan and general Black American expression

meanings.meaning: Something boring, tedious, or depressing
meanings.context: 1970s general slang

meanings.meaning: An authority figure — the police, government, or anyone in power keeping you down
meanings.context: 1970s counterculture / Black American expression

meanings.meaning: To relax; to hang out without doing much
meanings.context: 1970s — became a permanent fixture in American slang

meanings.meaning: To sleep, especially at someone else's place; to stay somewhere temporarily
meanings.context: 1970s counterculture slang

meanings.meaning: Used after a statement to reveal it was a joke; 'just kidding!'
meanings.context: 1970s–1980s teen culture (also spelled 'psych')

meanings.meaning: Shoplifting; stealing something from a store
meanings.context: 1970s street slang — your 'five fingers' are the discount method

meanings.meaning: Short for radical; cool, awesome, excellent

meanings.meaning: Cool, excellent, intense; or difficult/dangerous

meanings.meaning: Remarkable, breathtaking, totally cool

meanings.meaning: Excellent, attractive, remarkable

meanings.meaning: Fake, not cool, unacceptable

meanings.meaning: An exclamation of excitement or enthusiasm

meanings.meaning: A socially awkward or studious person; a nerd

meanings.meaning: A silly, foolish, or unintelligent person

meanings.meaning: A person who spends excessive time hanging out at the shopping mall

meanings.meaning: Extremely excited, thrilled

meanings.meaning: Calm down; relax; stop overreacting

meanings.meaning: Absolutely; completely; used as an intensifier

meanings.meaning: Expression of disgust or strong disapproval

meanings.meaning: Gross, disgusting; 'grody to the max' means extremely gross

meanings.meaning: What's wrong with you? What's your problem?

meanings.meaning: Tough, formidable, not to be messed with

meanings.meaning: Someone who dresses in clean-cut, upper-class style; Polo shirts, khakis

meanings.meaning: Going ballistic means completely losing your temper

meanings.meaning: To overreact; to get extremely upset about something

meanings.meaning: To leave; to go
meanings.context: 1980s–1990s hip-hop/street culture

meanings.meaning: Excellent, amazing, very impressive

meanings.meaning: Excellent, cool, attractive; sometimes said to mean 'Pretty Hot And Tempting'
meanings.context: 1990s hip-hop culture — not related to 'fat'

meanings.meaning: Respect, credit, recognition for something well done

meanings.meaning: I don't want to hear it; dismissive gesture of holding up your palm

meanings.meaning: Flashy, expensive jewelry or accessories; ostentatious wealth display

meanings.meaning: Cool, awesome, excellent
meanings.context: 1990s hip-hop culture — originally meant drugs, flipped to mean great

meanings.meaning: Stupid or foolish (older meaning)
meanings.context: Pre-1990s usage still exists in some contexts

meanings.meaning: Used at the end of a statement to reverse/negate it sarcastically

meanings.meaning: Great, excellent, impressive

meanings.meaning: Excellent, cool, well-done

meanings.meaning: An exclamation of triumph, excitement, or celebration

meanings.meaning: Sexually attractive; having an appealing body

meanings.meaning: Chill and relax combined; to calm down completely
meanings.context: 2000s — portmanteau that became mainstream teen slang

meanings.meaning: Very; a lot; extremely

meanings.meaning: Of poor quality; unreliable; sketchy
meanings.context: 2000s general slang

meanings.meaning: A newcomer; someone inexperienced

meanings.meaning: Not Safe For Work — content inappropriate for a professional setting
meanings.context: 2000s internet culture — a content warning label

meanings.meaning: Completely dominated or defeated; owned

meanings.meaning: Bitter, upset, or resentful about something

meanings.meaning: Friends; your people
meanings.context: 2000s general slang

meanings.meaning: To call out to someone; to get in touch; a greeting
meanings.context: 2000s hip-hop culture

meanings.meaning: An extremely enthusiastic fan, often to an obsessive degree

meanings.meaning: Acting like a responsible adult; doing grown-up tasks like paying bills
meanings.context: 2010s millennial culture — treating adulthood as an achievement

meanings.meaning: Romantic partner; term of endearment; 'Before Anyone Else'
meanings.context: 2010s — popularized on social media and in music

meanings.meaning: High-class, luxurious, aspiring to a fancy lifestyle
meanings.context: 2010s — from 'bourgeoisie'; popularized by Migos' 'Bad and Boujee' (2016)

meanings.meaning: To reject, boycott, or publicly withdraw support from someone
meanings.context: 2010s — 'cancel culture' became a major social phenomenon

meanings.meaning: Over the top; excessive; dramatic; doing too much
meanings.context: 2010s social media culture — being 'extra' can be fun or annoying

meanings.meaning: Perfectly done; flawlessly styled

meanings.meaning: To suddenly cut off all communication with someone without explanation
meanings.context: 2010s dating/social media culture — you vanish like a ghost

meanings.meaning: Openly, obviously; the opposite of 'low key'
meanings.context: 2010s — used to emphasize something publicly

meanings.meaning: Amazing, exciting, fun; originally meant intoxicated
meanings.context: 2010s hip-hop/party culture that went mainstream

meanings.meaning: Subtly, secretly; understated; quietly
meanings.context: 2010s — used to downplay or soften a statement

meanings.meaning: A relatable feeling or situation; 'that's exactly how I feel'
meanings.context: 2010s social media culture — reacting to content that resonates

meanings.meaning: Proof or evidence, usually screenshots or documentation
meanings.context: 2010s social media culture — 'show me the receipts' means prove it

meanings.meaning: Bold, ruthless, unapologetically harsh; doing something without caring what others think
meanings.context: 2010s — popularized by Megan Thee Stallion's 'Savage' (2020)

meanings.meaning: Shocked, surprised, emotionally rattled
meanings.context: 2010s — from AAVE; went mainstream via social media/Mobb Deep references

meanings.meaning: To subtly insult or show disrespect to someone
meanings.context: 2010s — from Black/LGBTQ+ ballroom culture; popularized by 'Paris Is Burning' documentary

meanings.meaning: Okay, yes, sounds good; expression of agreement or confirmation
meanings.context: 2020s Gen Z — from AAVE, became widespread through TikTok and texting

meanings.meaning: A lie or falsehood (as in 'that's cap' = 'that's a lie')
meanings.context: 2020s Gen Z — from AAVE/hip-hop; 'cap' = lie, 'no cap' = no lie

meanings.meaning: No lie; for real; I'm being completely honest
meanings.context: 2020s Gen Z — from AAVE/hip-hop culture

meanings.meaning: Out of date, trying too hard; trends that are no longer cool, often associated with millennial aesthetics
meanings.context: 2020s Gen Z — coined by Gaby Rasson in 2013, went viral on TikTok in 2021

meanings.meaning: Stylish, fashionable; especially referring to clothing and overall swag
meanings.context: 2020s — from hip-hop culture; 'dripping' with style

meanings.meaning: Feels uniquely special or emotionally significant; not like other things
meanings.context: 2020s Gen Z — used for experiences that feel more meaningful than usual

meanings.meaning: Describes something as excellent, especially music; 'this song slaps'
meanings.context: 2020s — from music culture; something that hits hard

meanings.meaning: Gossip, news, or juicy information; 'spill the tea' means share the gossip
meanings.context: 2020s — from Black/LGBTQ+ ballroom culture ('T' for truth); went mainstream

meanings.meaning: Assessing someone's mood, energy, or the atmosphere of a situation
meanings.context: 2020s Gen Z — 'checking the vibe' of a person or place

meanings.meaning: To throw something forcefully; also an exclamation of excitement
meanings.context: 2020s — originally from a viral Vine video (2014), resurgent on TikTok

meanings.meaning: Charisma, especially romantic charm; the ability to attract someone
meanings.context: 2020s — coined/popularized by Kai Cenat and NYC streaming culture ~2022. Oxford word of the year 2023

meanings.meaning: Can be used as a verb: 'to rizz someone up' means to charm them
meanings.context: Extended usage in Gen Z dating culture

meanings.meaning: The ability to attract someone without speaking — through looks, body language, or pure presence
meanings.context: Extension of 'rizz'; TikTok trend 2023

meanings.meaning: Suspicious or shady; something seems off or untrustworthy
meanings.context: 2020s — popularized by the game Among Us (2020) where you identify 'sus' impostors

meanings.meaning: Extremely good, especially food; delicious; amazing
meanings.context: 2020s — from AAVE; went viral on TikTok food reviews

meanings.meaning: Going to; about to; planning to ('fixing to')
meanings.context: From AAVE — contraction of 'fixing to'; used for decades in Black communities, went mainstream 2020s

meanings.meaning: To do something exceptionally well; to kill it; to look amazing
meanings.context: 2020s mainstream — originally from Black/LGBTQ+ ballroom culture dating back decades

meanings.meaning: Used to emphasize a definitive statement; 'that's final' or 'end of discussion'
meanings.context: 2020s — from AAVE/drag culture; often styled as 'periodt' with a hard T

meanings.meaning: A humorous/exasperated way to say 'child' — expressing disbelief or weariness
meanings.context: From AAVE — 'chile' pronunciation of 'child'; popularized on social media

meanings.meaning: I swear; I'm being dead serious; truthfully
meanings.context: 2020s — from AAVE; a strong affirmation of honesty

meanings.meaning: Did something perfectly; absolutely nailed it (past tense of 'eat')
meanings.context: 2020s — from Black/LGBTQ+ culture: 'she ate that' or 'ate and left no crumbs'

meanings.meaning: Did something so perfectly there's nothing left to criticize
meanings.context: 2020s — extension of 'ate'; from drag/ballroom culture

meanings.meaning: Delusional; having unrealistic expectations, especially about relationships or K-pop idols
meanings.context: 2020s — originally from K-pop fan culture, went mainstream on TikTok

meanings.meaning: Caught red-handed with undeniable evidence; exposed with proof
meanings.context: 2020s Gen Z — '4K' referring to high-definition video capturing every detail

meanings.meaning: It's reminiscent of; it has the energy/vibe of something
meanings.context: 2020s — from AAVE/ballroom culture: 'it's giving boss energy'

meanings.meaning: Someone who seems to act without independent thought; a background character in real life
meanings.context: 2020s — from gaming (Non-Player Character); applied to people who seem scripted

meanings.meaning: Something you think about constantly; an obsession
meanings.context: 2020s TikTok trend — 'how often do you think about the Roman Empire?' revealed men think about it daily

meanings.meaning: A win; something positive or successful
meanings.context: 2020s internet/gaming culture — abbreviation of 'Win'

meanings.meaning: A loss; something negative or embarrassing
meanings.context: 2020s internet/gaming culture — abbreviation of 'Loss'

meanings.meaning: Unapologetically being yourself; having a bold, authentic opinion regardless of what others think
meanings.context: 2020s internet culture — popularized by rapper Lil B 'The Based God'; adopted by meme culture

meanings.meaning: When a reply gets more likes than the original post — meaning the crowd disagrees with the OP
meanings.context: 2020s Twitter/X culture — 'you got ratio'd'

meanings.meaning: Someone or something occupying your thoughts constantly without effort
meanings.context: 2020s internet culture — 'living rent free in my head'

meanings.meaning: Acting like or being the protagonist of a story; someone who stands out
meanings.context: 2020s TikTok — 'main character energy' means you're the star of your own life
meanings.tone: Admiring or mocking depending on context

meanings.meaning: Perfectly executed what was expected; nailed it
meanings.context: 2020s social media — used for someone who delivered exactly right

meanings.meaning: Average, mediocre, underwhelming; nothing special
meanings.context: 2020s internet/gaming culture — the ultimate insult for something that's just okay

meanings.meaning: An extremely devoted fan; to be a passionate supporter of someone
meanings.context: 2020s mainstream — from Eminem's song 'Stan' (2000) about an obsessive fan

meanings.meaning: Someone who does too much for a person they like, often without reciprocation
meanings.context: 2020s internet culture — used primarily about men going overboard for attention

meanings.meaning: A positive sign or good quality in a person, especially in dating
meanings.context: 2020s dating/TikTok culture — opposite of 'red flag'

meanings.meaning: A nonsense/meme word associated with chaotic energy; from 'Skibidi Toilet' YouTube series
meanings.context: 2023+ Gen Alpha — from DaFuq!?Boom!'s viral animated series on YouTube

meanings.meaning: An independent, self-reliant person who doesn't follow the crowd; a lone wolf
meanings.context: 2020s internet — from the 'sigma male' meme/grindset culture
meanings.tone: Admiring or ironic depending on context

meanings.meaning: An exclamation of surprise at an attractive person, especially their figure
meanings.context: 2023+ Gen Alpha/Gen Z — from streamer YourRAGE's pronunciation of 'god damn'

meanings.meaning: Used as a stand-in for a strange, bizarre, or cursed place ('only in Ohio')
meanings.context: 2023+ Gen Alpha meme — 'Ohio is the most cursed state' became a running joke

meanings.meaning: Taking a portion of someone's food without asking
meanings.context: 2023+ Gen Alpha — from streamer Fanum's habit of taking bites of friends' food on stream

meanings.meaning: A jawline exercise where you press your tongue to the roof of your mouth; used as a meme about improving appearance
meanings.context: 2023+ Gen Alpha/Gen Z — from orthodontist John Mew's tongue posture technique, turned meme

meanings.meaning: Brother; mate; a casual term of address
meanings.context: British English — shortened form of 'brother'; common in London multicultural English (MLE)

meanings.meaning: Very attractive; good-looking; can also mean excellent
meanings.context: British slang — from Multicultural London English; possibly Jamaican influence

meanings.meaning: Isn't it? / Right? — used to seek confirmation or emphasize a point
meanings.context: British English — contraction of 'isn't it'; universal tag question in UK slang

meanings.meaning: A group of male friends; the boys; your crew
meanings.context: British/London slang — from Jamaican Patois influence

meanings.meaning: Very; a lot of; extremely
meanings.context: British/London slang — 'bare jokes' means 'very funny' or 'a lot of jokes'

meanings.meaning: Let it go; forget about it; stop doing that
meanings.context: British/London slang — 'allow that' means don't bother or cut it out

meanings.meaning: A useless, worthless person; someone who wastes their potential
meanings.context: British/London slang — strong insult in UK youth culture

meanings.meaning: Mildly disrespectful in a charming way; impudent but endearing; also means spontaneous
meanings.context: British English — 'a cheeky Nando's' means an impromptu visit to Nando's restaurant

meanings.meaning: Short for banter; playful, teasing conversation between friends
meanings.context: British English — 'top bants' or 'good bants' means excellent playful chat

meanings.meaning: A man; a guy
meanings.context: British English — very common everyday word for a male person

meanings.meaning: Extremely disappointed or upset
meanings.context: British English — 'I'm absolutely gutted' means devastated

meanings.meaning: Wealthy; having lots of money
meanings.context: British English — 'he's minted' means he's rich

meanings.meaning: Suspicious, unreliable, or of questionable quality
meanings.context: British English — 'a dodgy geezer' is an untrustworthy person

meanings.meaning: Arranged; taken care of; all good
meanings.context: British English — 'we're sorted' means everything is handled

meanings.meaning: Good Game — said at the end of a match as sportsmanship (or sometimes sarcastically)
meanings.context: Gaming culture — universal across all competitive gaming

meanings.meaning: Away From Keyboard — temporarily unavailable
meanings.context: Gaming/internet culture — used when stepping away mid-session

meanings.meaning: To weaken something in a game update; to reduce power or effectiveness
meanings.context: Gaming culture — from Nerf brand toy guns (soft/harmless)

meanings.meaning: To strengthen something in a game update; to increase power or effectiveness
meanings.context: Gaming culture — opposite of 'nerf'

meanings.meaning: Overpowered — something unfairly strong or dominant in a game
meanings.context: Gaming culture — used when something is too powerful and unbalanced

meanings.meaning: Original Poster — the person who started a thread or post
meanings.context: Internet/Reddit culture

meanings.meaning: A new or inexperienced player; someone who plays badly
meanings.context: Gaming culture — from 'newbie'; variations: n00b, newb

meanings.meaning: Making a critical play under pressure; coming through when it matters most
meanings.context: Gaming/sports culture — 'he clutched the round' means he won when it seemed impossible

meanings.meaning: Most Effective Tactics Available; the dominant strategy in a game
meanings.context: Gaming culture — 'playing the meta' means using the best-known strategy

meanings.meaning: Intentionally ruining other players' experience; trolling in-game
meanings.context: Gaming culture — 'griefers' deliberately harass other players

meanings.meaning: An expression of excitement or amazement (from the PogChamp Twitch emote)
meanings.context: 2020s Twitch/gaming streaming culture

meanings.meaning: Go outside; get off the internet; you're too online
meanings.context: 2020s gaming/internet culture — telling someone to reconnect with reality

meanings.meaning: Trying extremely hard in a casual game; being overly competitive
meanings.context: Gaming culture — 'sweats' are players who try-hard in non-competitive modes

meanings.meaning: Displaying negative, harmful, or hostile behavior in games or online
meanings.context: Gaming culture that spread to general usage — 'toxic player' or 'toxic relationship'

meanings.meaning: Your favorite member of a K-pop group; the one you're most drawn to
meanings.context: K-pop fandom culture — everyone has a 'bias' in their favorite group

meanings.meaning: Acting cute or childish to charm others — exaggerated cuteness
meanings.context: Korean culture / K-pop — idols often do aegyo as fanservice

meanings.meaning: A selfie (Korean: 셀카, from 'self camera')
meanings.context: Korean/K-pop culture — fans and idols share selcas

meanings.meaning: Amazing; awesome; jackpot (Korean: 대박)
meanings.context: Korean culture — used to express amazement or that something is incredible

meanings.meaning: An expression of encouragement; 'you can do it!' (Korean: 화이팅 hwaiting)
meanings.context: Korean culture — used across K-pop, K-drama, and everyday Korean life

meanings.meaning: The youngest member of a K-pop group (Korean: 막내)
meanings.context: K-pop culture — the maknae often has a special role/dynamic in the group

meanings.meaning: When a K-pop group releases new music and performs it; a new album cycle
meanings.context: K-pop industry term — not a 'return' per se, just a new release cycle

meanings.meaning: The designated most attractive member of a K-pop group
meanings.context: K-pop industry — a formal role/position within group dynamics

meanings.meaning: An obsessive, invasive fan who crosses personal boundaries and stalks idols
meanings.context: K-pop culture — from Korean 사생 meaning 'private life'; a very negative term

meanings.meaning: A K-pop performer/celebrity; trained and debuted by an entertainment company
meanings.context: K-pop industry term — distinct from Western 'celebrity'

meanings.meaning: Your ultimate favorite; your #1 bias across all K-pop groups
meanings.context: K-pop fandom — short for 'ultimate bias'

meanings.meaning: Cool, great, awesome
meanings.context: Latin American Spanish — widely used in Venezuela, Colombia, Ecuador, Caribbean

meanings.meaning: Truth; for real; 'neta?' = 'really?'
meanings.context: Mexican Spanish slang — very common in everyday Mexican speech

meanings.meaning: A close friend; buddy; bro
meanings.context: Venezuelan/Colombian Spanish slang

meanings.meaning: Cool, nice, awesome
meanings.context: Mexican Spanish slang — one of the most common expressions of approval in Mexico

meanings.meaning: Dude, man, bro (also spelled 'wey')
meanings.context: Mexican Spanish — extremely common; can be friendly or insulting depending on tone

meanings.meaning: Cool, great, awesome
meanings.context: South American Spanish — used in Peru, Chile, Colombia

meanings.meaning: Okay, let's go, sure; expression of agreement
meanings.context: Latin American Spanish — also popularized by Pitbull's catchphrase 'Dale!'

meanings.meaning: Afternoon
meanings.context: Australian English — Australians shorten everything and add -o or -ie

meanings.meaning: Breakfast
meanings.context: Australian English — shortened with -ie suffix

meanings.meaning: To vomit; to throw up
meanings.context: Australian English — possibly from 'watch under' (a warning on ships)

meanings.meaning: A gas station / petrol station
meanings.context: Australian English — shortened form of 'service station'

meanings.meaning: Sunglasses
meanings.context: Australian English — another -ie diminutive

meanings.meaning: Work, especially hard physical work
meanings.context: Australian English — from Yagara Aboriginal language

meanings.meaning: That's okay; you're welcome; it's not a problem
meanings.context: Australian English — Australia's unofficial national phrase; the ultimate casual reassurance

meanings.meaning: To think or believe; 'I reckon' = 'I think' or 'definitely'
meanings.context: Australian English — extremely common in everyday speech

meanings.meaning: A lot; very much; tons of
meanings.context: Australian English — 'heaps good' means 'very good'

meanings.meaning: Friend; buddy; a term of address for anyone
meanings.context: Australian/British English — the most iconic Australian greeting

meanings.meaning: Flip-flops (not underwear)
meanings.context: Australian English — can cause confusion with American English

meanings.meaning: Presenting yourself exceptionally well; looking incredible
meanings.context: LGBTQ+/ballroom culture — 'she's serving looks' means she looks amazing

meanings.meaning: Intensifier meaning 'extremely well' — 'boots the house down'
meanings.context: LGBTQ+/ballroom culture — originated in NYC ball scene

meanings.meaning: A revered figure; someone who embodies elegance, power, and care for their community
meanings.context: LGBTQ+/ballroom culture — the mother of a 'house' in ball culture; used for icons

meanings.meaning: Having an amazing time; thriving; in your best state
meanings.context: LGBTQ+ culture — 'I'm living for this' means it's giving you life

meanings.meaning: Looking exceptionally good; perfectly styled; can also mean 'shook'
meanings.context: LGBTQ+/ballroom culture — 'your wig is snatched' (you look stunning or are shocked)

meanings.meaning: Expression of being so amazed your wig flies off; mind-blown
meanings.context: LGBTQ+/drag culture — 'wig!' = 'I'm shook!'

meanings.meaning: Go off! Do your thing! An encouragement to perform or show out
meanings.context: LGBTQ+/ballroom/drag culture — 'werk it!' is the ultimate hype

meanings.meaning: A blend of 'honey' and a strong expletive; a term of endearment or warning depending on tone
meanings.context: LGBTQ+/drag culture — popularized by 'RuPaul's Drag Race'

meanings.meaning: An attractive masculine person, especially one who doesn't openly identify as queer
meanings.context: LGBTQ+ culture — originally from ballroom/Polari slang

meanings.meaning: A casual gathering or party with friends; gossip session; laughing and having fun
meanings.context: LGBTQ+/ballroom culture — 'having a kiki' means casual fun hangs

meanings.meaning: Left speechless; stunned by something amazing
meanings.context: LGBTQ+/drag culture — 'I was gagged by that performance'

meanings.meaning: To publicly call out someone's flaws or insecurities with precision and wit
meanings.context: LGBTQ+/ballroom culture — 'she read him for filth' means a devastating callout

meanings.meaning: The truth; gossip; the real information
meanings.context: LGBTQ+/drag culture — evolved into mainstream 'spill the tea'

meanings.meaning: Diamonds or expensive jewelry; also means cool/chill demeanor
meanings.context: Hip-hop culture — 'iced out' means wearing lots of diamonds

meanings.meaning: To show off, especially wealth, skills, or possessions
meanings.context: Hip-hop culture — 'flexing on them' means showing off

meanings.meaning: Impressive rap lyrics; skilled lyrical content
meanings.context: Hip-hop culture — 'he spit some bars' means excellent rapping

meanings.meaning: Excellent, amazing, hot; something incredibly good
meanings.context: Hip-hop/internet culture — 'that track is fire' / 🔥 emoji

meanings.meaning: Something very good or impressive; also means a firearm
meanings.context: Hip-hop culture — 'that's heat' means it's great

meanings.meaning: A place where drugs are sold; also a subgenre of hip-hop music
meanings.context: Hip-hop culture — 'trap house' or 'trap music' from Atlanta

meanings.meaning: Influence, fame, or social status; 'clout chasing' means seeking attention/fame
meanings.context: Hip-hop/internet culture — 'she's got clout' means she has influence

meanings.meaning: A car, especially a nice one
meanings.context: Hip-hop culture — 'nice whip' means nice car

meanings.meaning: Hilarious; something so funny you're figuratively dead from laughing
meanings.context: Hip-hop/internet culture — 'I'm dead 💀' = that's hilarious

meanings.meaning: To handle something with skill and charm; to smoothly get what you want
meanings.context: Hip-hop culture — 'finessed it' means pulled it off with style

meanings.meaning: Cap = lie, no cap = no lie / for real
meanings.context: Hip-hop/AAVE — 'stop cappin' means stop lying

meanings.meaning: To give maximum effort; to perform intensely
meanings.context: Hip-hop culture — 'go hard or go home'

meanings.meaning: Exceptionally good; impressively skilled; ruthless in a positive way
meanings.context: Hip-hop/AAVE — 'that verse was cold' means it was devastatingly good
